providerExtensions.name: Three transport mechanisms
providerExtensions.description: Gemini CLI supports three MCP transport types: stdio (spawns a subprocess, communicates via stdin/stdout, configured via command + optional args/cwd), SSE (Server-Sent Events, configured via url), and Streamable HTTP (configured via httpUrl). The transport type can be set explicitly via the type field (stdio/sse/http) or inferred from which connection property is present.

providerExtensions.name: Per-server tool allowlist and blocklist
providerExtensions.description: Each MCP server config supports includeTools (string[]) to allowlist specific tools from that server, and excludeTools (string[]) to blocklist tools. excludeTools takes precedence over includeTools — if a tool appears in both, it is excluded. When includeTools is omitted, all tools from the server are available.

providerExtensions.name: Per-server trust flag
providerExtensions.description: The trust boolean on an MCP server configuration bypasses all tool call confirmations for that server when set to true (default: false). Trusted servers execute tools without prompting the user for approval.

providerExtensions.name: Global MCP allowed/excluded lists
providerExtensions.description: The top-level mcp object in settings.json provides global rules across all servers: mcp.allowed (string[]) allowlists server names so only listed servers connect; mcp.excluded (string[]) blocklists server names to prevent connection. These are global filters applied before per-server configuration.

providerExtensions.name: Environment variable expansion in env blocks
providerExtensions.description: Gemini CLI automatically expands environment variables in the env block of MCP server configs. Supports POSIX/Bash syntax ($VAR or ${VAR}) on all platforms and Windows syntax (%VAR%) on Windows. This allows referencing secrets from the shell environment without hardcoding them in settings.json.

providerExtensions.name: CLI commands for MCP server management
providerExtensions.description: MCP servers can be managed via gemini mcp subcommands: mcp add (add a server), mcp remove (delete from config), mcp list (view status of all servers), mcp enable/disable (toggle without removing, with optional --session flag for session-only changes). Enable/disable state is persisted to ~/.gemini/mcp-server-enablement.json. In-session equivalents: /mcp list, /mcp enable <name>, /mcp disable <name>, /mcp reload.

providerExtensions.name: MCP resource referencing via @ syntax
providerExtensions.description: Gemini CLI discovers MCP server resources (via resources/list) at connection time and exposes them alongside filesystem paths in the @ completion menu. Resources are referenced with @server://resource/path syntax in conversation. The CLI calls resources/read and injects the content into the conversation when referenced.
